name: Current Affairs in Science & Technology (2): Padma Awards 2026 & Infosys Prize 2025

sourceRefs.label: vajiramandravi.com/PIB - Padma Awards 2026 breakdown and science recipients
infosys.com - Infosys Prize 2025 laureates and prize structure

researchNotes: Second lap of the Part A Current-Affairs rotation begins (back to Unit I). Picked award announcements — concrete, dated, and stable once announced — over volatile ongoing-research statistics, continuing the lesson logged on Day 58 about preferring durable current-affairs facts.

reading.intro: Covers the Padma Awards 2026 (announced on the eve of Republic Day 2026) and the Infosys Prize 2025 — India's major civilian and scientific recognition programmes.

qa.question: How many total Padma Awards were announced for 2026, and what is the breakdown by category?
qa.answer: 131 total awards: 5 Padma Vibhushan, 13 Padma Bhushan, and 113 Padma Shri — announced on the eve of Republic Day 2026.

qa.question: What were the demographic highlights of the Padma Awards 2026 list?
qa.answer: 19 women awardees; 6 in the Foreigners/NRI/PIO/OCI category; and 16 posthumous awards.

qa.question: Name two Science & Engineering Padma Shri 2026 awardees and their states.
What is the Infosys Prize, and what does each laureate receive?
qa.answer: An annual award from the Infosys Science Foundation, given across six categories (Economics, Engineering & Computer Science, Humanities & Social Sciences, Life Sciences, Mathematical Sciences, Physical Sciences). Each winner receives a gold medal, a citation, and USD 100,000.

qa.question: What notable later achievement is associated with past Infosys Prize laureates, illustrating the prize's track record?
qa.answer: Past laureates have gone on to win the Nobel Prize (e.g., Abhijit Banerjee and Esther Duflo) and the Fields Medal (e.g., Manjul Bhargava and Akshay Venkatesh) — underlining the Infosys Prize's reputation as an early indicator of major international scientific recognition.
